name: Carriage 2 — The Conductor's Office
summary: A narrow office of dark wood and brass, paper stacked to the height of a standing man, and the Conductor seated at the desk as if you were on the manifest for this exact hour.

notable_features: Stacks of bound manifests rise from the floor to chest height along the side wall. The paper is the colour of paper that has been kept for a long time. Each spine has a date. None of the dates are this year.

A route map is pinned above the desk. The line of the Train doubles back on itself in three places. At two of those crossings the paper is worn through, as if someone has put a thumb there often enough to wear it thin.

The Conductor is in the chair facing the door. He does not stand. The cap shades the upper half of his face. His hands are folded on a closed ledger, and the ledger is bound in something darker than leather.
On the desk corner, a brass bell. It has no clapper. There is a notch in the rim where one used to be.

The forward door is a slab of iron with no handle. A keyhole at chest height, large enough to take a key the length of a finger. The keyhole has been used recently. The brass around it is the brass of being touched.

san_triggers.trigger_text: Somewhere in the stacks is a manifest with your name on it, in a hand you do not recognise.
The Train's line on the map crosses itself. It has been doing so for longer than this Train has been moving.
The Conductor is not still. He is positioned. The two are different and you can tell which.

skill_check_hooks.action_hint: find your name in the manifests
read the Conductor's expression under the cap
speak to the Conductor with the right fragment

puzzle.description: The Conductor does not refuse passage. He requires that the investigator name what they have learned. Each fragment recovered earlier becomes a key turn.
puzzle.unlock_condition: Player offers at least three fragments_heard to the Conductor in dialogue AND passes a Persuade (hard) OR Psychology (hard) check, OR presents the assembled key-fragment items.
